english: (قبضه Ar.) W. hilt, handle of a sword.
(كفش Pers.) shoe; in W. esp. the leather shoes of Hindu fashion, which are also bought by wealthier Tibetans.
Ssk. n. of a country in the northwest of India, Wdn.: kam-po-rtse (kam po rtse).
watermelon Sch.
also kar-kar (kar kar), great pain, suffering Lex.
Ssk. the constellation of Cancer.
loan, when respectfully requested, cf skyin (skyin).
register, list.
Ssk. ('deed, action'); kar-ma-pa (kar ma pa) (in Nepal karmika) name of a philosophical school of Buddhism.
porcelain. china-ware, -cup etc.
also kér-laṅ-ba (ker lang ba), to stand up, to rise.

wylie: kar sha pa Ni or Na
english: Ssk. कार्षापण, a coin in ancient India, or rather a weight of gold and silver, of different value (not = 'cowries', as Sch. seems to think).
also kal-yór (kal yor), W. col. instead of kar-yól (kar yol), the former seems to be a corruption of قلعی.
numerical figure: 31, ki-pa (ki pa) the 31st (volume).
wild leek Sch.
n. of the vowel-sign for i, ི.
Dzl. ༢༡༩ acc. to Schf. a corruption of the Chinese khin, a lyre with 7 strings. (Pilgrim. of Fa-Hian Calc. 1848 p. 265).
tickling W., *ki-tsí kúg-c̣e* (*ki tsi kug che*) to tickle.
v. kaṅ-daṅ-kiṅ (kang dang king).
n. of a fruit, Lex.
numerical figure: 61; kú-pa (ku pa) the 61st (volume).
kú-sgra (ku sgra) B., *kú-c̣o* (*ku cho*) W clamour.
Ssk., n. of a bird in the Himalaya.
Wdn. gourd.
Ssk. the god of riches, also Nag-ku-bera (nag ku be ra), Rnam-tós-kyi-bú (rnam thos kyi bu), Rnam-tós-srás (rnam thos sras), Lag-na-rdó-rje (lag na rdo rje) etc.
Ssk. the flower of the red and white lotus, Nymphaea rubra and esculenta.
sediment of urine Med.
n. of a female deity Mil.
Ld. colt of an ass.
also ku-res (ku res) jest, joke, ku-re byed-pa (ku re byed pa) to jest, cf. kyal-ka (kyal ka).

key, also lock; more accurately: *pé-ku-lig* (*pe ku lig*) key, c̣úy-ku-lig (chuy ku lig) lock, padlock; *ku-lig-búr-* (*ku lig bur-*) (or bor- (bor-)) tse* (tse*) in Ld. a contrivance used instead of a doorlatch. W.
a sort of grass, Poa cynosuroides, often used in sacred ceremonies.
apple Dzl.; W. (Cf. sli (sli)).
Ssk. flower.
ring-dove Cs. (Ssk.: the cry of the cuckoo).

also kug-kúg (kug kug), crooked; a hook; gri-kug (gri kug) a curved knife, short sabre; lcags-kug (lcags kug) an iron hook; nya-kug (nya kug) a fishing-hook; *kug-kug jhé-pa* (*kug kug jhe pa*) C. *taṅ-c̣e* (*tang che*) W. to bend, curve; clinch (a nail); *go kug taṅ-c̣e* (*go kug tang che*) W. to nod, *lag kug taṅ-c̣e* (*lag kug tang che*) W. to beckon. (Cf. kum-pa (kum pa).)
*kug-se* (*kug se*) cuckoo W.
